title: 10 best herbal tinctures for nausea

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which work to calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness, morning sickness, and chemotherapy. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, bind to receptors in the stomach, reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive tract. This results in a reduction of nausea and vomiting, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable digestive process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating nausea and discomfort.

Digestive enzymes: Ginger stimulates the production of digestive enzymes in the stomach, such as gastric amylase and lipase, which helps to break down food more efficiently and absorb nutrients properly, reducing nausea and bloating.

Antioxidants and free radical scavengers: Ginger's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the digestive system, contributing to nausea and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate nausea.

Rhyzomes: As the storage organ of the ginger plant, rhyzomes contain concentrated amounts of essential oils and bioactive compounds that are specifically designed to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality zingiber officinale rhizomes at the peak of freshness and cleanliness.
Chop fresh or dried ginger root into small pieces ensuring uniform size for optimal extraction.
Combine one part chopped ginger with five parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for four weeks shaking daily, ensuring complete extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and transferring tincture to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and calming digestive issues. The menthol content in peppermint oil also has a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce spasms and cramping that can contribute to nausea. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint may help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which is often linked with nausea. By using herbal peppermint tinctures, individuals can naturally alleviate nausea and promote a sense of calm and digestive comfort.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint oil in tincture form can stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, helping to relieve nausea caused by slow or blocked digestive systems.
Anti-Inflammatory: The menthol content in peppermint tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining that may be contributing to nausea symptoms.

Gastroprotective Agent: Peppermint tincture's antacid and anti-inflammatory properties also offer gastroprotective effects, providing relief from nausea caused by acid reflux, stomach ulcers, or other digestive disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea symptoms.
Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves mentha x piperita with scissors cutting stems at soil level early morning.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 1-2 weeks.
Combine dried peppermint leaves with 80 proof vodka filling glass jar leaving 1-inch headspace.
Steep mixture in dark cabinet for 6-8 weeks shaking jar twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the stomach and alleviate digestive discomfort. The herb's soothing properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting. Additionally, lemongrass contains compounds that have a gentle sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to ease anxiety and stress-related nausea. By promoting relaxation and reducing digestive upset, lemongrass tinctures can provide fast and effective relief from nausea symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Lemongrass tincture helps to dispel gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate nausea symptoms by allowing for easier digestion and reducing pressure on the stomach.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea caused by gastrointestinal issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of lemongrass tincture can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass contain a high concentration of essential oils, which makes them ideal for extracting into a tincture to help alleviate nausea.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired yield of tincture.
Chop the harvested lemongrass into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of one part herb to four parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, pressing on the solids to extract as much tincture as possible.
Transfer the resulting herbal lemongrass tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Papaya tinctures helps with nausea because it contains papain, an enzyme that breaks down proteins and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract. This anti-inflammatory property helps to soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of nausea. Additionally, papaya's natural antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive system, allowing for smoother digestion and reducing feelings of queasiness. By calming the stomach and promoting a healthy gut environment, papaya tinctures provide natural relief from nausea and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Papain: Papain, an enzyme found in papaya, helps to break down proteins and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Vitamin C: Vitamin C in papaya tincture has antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals that may contribute to nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
Fiber: The fiber content of papaya tincture can help to regulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and soothe the digestive tract, all of which can help alleviate nausea and related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Papaya roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of nausea due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: The ripe fruits of the papaya plant contain papain, an enzyme that helps break down protein and ease digestion, making them effective in treating nausea related to indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carica papaya fruit and wash it thoroughly under running water.
Dry the papaya slices using a food dehydrator or sun drying method for 12-24 hours.
Grind dried papaya into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of powdered papaya with 100 ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the tincture mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds. The tannins, flavonoids, and alkaloids present in the tree's leaves have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms of nausea. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree's natural ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation may also contribute to its effectiveness in alleviating nausea and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage to stomach lining caused by nausea-inducing substances, alleviating symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve blood flow to the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues like gastritis or motion sickness.
GABA-like activity: Ginkgo's GABA-mimetic properties can help regulate nervous system activity, calming anxiety and stress-induced nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of queasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds that have been shown to have anti-emetic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming stomach upset.
Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair Tree contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Cleanly wash and chop 500g of ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 1000ml of high-proof vodka and let steep for 14 days at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using the recommended dosage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique ability to calm the digestive system and ease stomach upset. The calming properties of lavender work to soothe the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to feelings of queasiness. Additionally, lavender's natural sedative effects help to quiet the mind and reduce stress, further alleviating nausea symptoms. As a result, English lavender tinctures provide a gentle and effective remedy for those experiencing nausea.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: English Lavender tincture helps to relax the digestive muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with nausea and vomiting.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: The calming effects of lavender can help alleviate anxiety-related nausea, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stress levels that contribute to nausea.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting by irritating the stomach lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, including linalool and camphor, which have been shown to have anti-nausea properties.
Seeds: English lavender seeds are rich in saponins, which have been found to be effective in reducing nausea and vomiting due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh english lavender flowers lavandula angustifolia, or dried ones if available, in sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Clean and dry the harvested lavender flowers thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the cleaned lavender flowers into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle to increase surface area for extraction.
Mix one part chopped lavender flowers with five parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, ensuring all material is submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking occasionally allowing time for solvents to extract constituents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to soothe the stomach and calm the digestive system. The apigenin and luteolin in chamomile work to reduce inflammation and relax muscle contractions, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the anti-anxiety effects of chamomile tincture can help to reduce stress and anxiety-induced nausea. By promoting relaxation and calming the stomach, chamomile tinctures provide a natural remedy for alleviating nausea and restoring digestive balance.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects can help alleviate nausea by calming the nervous system and reducing feelings of unease.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects can help soothe stomach muscles, reduce spasms, and ease nausea caused by digestive issues such as IBS or Crohn's disease.

Gastroprotective properties: Chamomile has been shown to possess gastroprotective effects, which means it can help shield the stomach lining from irritation and inflammation, reducing nausea and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make medicinal tinctures because they contain a higher concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make medicinal tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak bloom when petals are fully open for optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation until completely dry and crumbly.
Combine one part dried chamomile flowers with four parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the stomach and ease digestive discomfort. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, have been shown to reduce inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and bloating, further contributing to a sense of relief from nausea. As a result, herbal fennel tinctures can provide natural and effective relief for individuals experiencing nausea.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tincture's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can contribute to feelings of nausea.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause nausea.
Gastroprotective properties: Fennel tincture's gastroprotective properties help to protect the stomach lining from irritation, reducing inflammation and damage that can lead to nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to ease stomach discomfort and alleviate nausea, making them a popular choice for medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean high-quality dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems measuring one part by volume.
Combine one part of dried foeniculum vulgare with three parts of 95% ethanol vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for eight to twelve weeks allowing maceration.
After maceration strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass bottles filling them to the top and storing in a cool dark place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The root of the licorice plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, licorice tincture's sweet flavor can be soothing to the taste buds and provide a sense of relief from nausea symptoms. remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to nausea.

Flavonoids: Licorice tinctures contain flavonoids such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing nausea and vomiting.

Phlegm- dispelling properties: Licorice root has a long history of use in traditional Chinese medicine for its ability to dispel phlegm (mucus) from the body, which can become trapped in the digestive system and cause nausea, bloating, and other uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures for nausea.
Barks: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm stomach muscles and alleviate symptoms of nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots, wash them thoroughly and cut into small pieces.
Combine the root pieces with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part herb to 4 parts solvent for infusion.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar with a lid for 14 days in a cool dark place undisturbed.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass bottles and store it at room temperature protected from light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with nausea because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds that target the underlying causes of queasiness. The tincture's gentle stimulation of the digestive system helps to ease stomach upset and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate nausea caused by digestive issues.
Vasodilatory effects: The tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve blood flow to the digestive system, reducing cramping and discomfort that can exacerbate nausea.

Antioxidant and anti-oxidative stress properties: Dong Quai's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dong quai contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid and ligustilide, which have been shown to be effective in treating nausea and vomiting.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of dong quai is rich in polysaccharides, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried angelica sinensis roots from trusted sources.
Cut the roots into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 10 grams of root pieces with 100 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter and transfer it to amber glass bottles for long-term storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with nausea because it contains a unique blend of alkaloids and flavonoids that work together to calm the digestive system. The herb has been traditionally used to ease stomach upset, bloating, and cramping, which are often associated with nausea. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain a high concentration of glycosides, which can help to soothe and calm the digestive system, making them effective in reducing nausea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passionflower flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Chop 250 grams of the harvested passionflower flowers into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped passionflower flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking daily, to allow extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids, and label with date preparation time and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural sedative properties, which help to calm the stomach and reduce feelings of queasiness. The herbal remedy has been used for centuries to treat a range of digestive issues, including nausea, vomiting, and irritable bowel syndrome. By soothing the digestive tract and promoting relaxation, valerian tinctures can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and provide quick relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related nausea.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic compounds can help relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea.

Gastroprotective effects: Valerian has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation that may exacerbate nausea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.

Combine the chopped roots with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar leaving one inch at the top.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily to promote extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique composition of essential oils, specifically nepetalactone. This natural compound has been shown to interact with receptors in the brain that regulate vomiting and nausea. When ingested, nepetalactone binds to these receptors, calming the stomach and reducing feelings of queasiness. Additionally, catnip's sedative properties can also help relax the digestive system, further alleviating symptoms of nausea and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) effects: Catnip's anxiolytic properties can help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for nausea, allowing the body to focus on recovery rather than exacerbating symptoms.

Gastrointestinal soothing: Catnip's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds can help soothe and relax the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, cramping, and discomfort associated with nausea and vomiting.

Vomiting inhibition: Catnip's essential oils have been shown to inhibit vomiting in animal studies, which suggests that it may be effective in reducing the frequency and intensity of nausea-induced vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the catnip plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of catnip, also known as underground stems, are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried nepeta cataria herb in flower and leaf form for every three parts of high-proof vodka or ethanol at least 90%.
Combine the dry herb with the vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool dark place.
Allow the mixture to steep for four to six weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure all particles are saturated by the solvent.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator.
After two months transfer the tincture to dark glass dropper bottles with tight-fitting lids and label them clearly indicating the ingredients dosages preparations times and expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with nausea because they contain flavonoids and lupulin, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. These compounds also possess a sedative effect, helping to calm the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with nausea. remedy_properties: Xanthohumol: Hops contain a flavonoid called xanthohumol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of nausea.

Lupulone: Lupulone is a bitter compound found in hops that has been shown to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea by increasing the contraction of the stomach muscles and promoting the elimination of toxins from the digestive tract.

Humulus lupulus acids: The acids present in hops, particularly humulone and lupulone, have been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Hops buds are rich in essential oils that help to soothe the stomach and relieve symptoms of nausea, making them a popular choice for natural remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers or use dried forms of the plant.
Combine one part humulus lupulus with one part vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container, discarding solids after 20 minutes.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with nausea because it contains rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that has been shown to have potent anti-nausea properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for drying.
Dry the herbs in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at low heat for several hours.
Grind 1 part dried melissa officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle then mix with 5 parts high-proof vodka or neutral spirit.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking daily then strain through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and take 10-20 drops in water or under the tongue as needed to alleviate nausea.

Strain tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon verbena's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon verbena tincture may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can exacerbate nausea and vomiting.
Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lemon verbena's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and calming properties can help to soothe the nervous system and alleviate stress-related nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon verbena contain a high concentration of essential oils, which makes them effective in calming stomach upset and reducing nausea.
Buds: Lemon verbena buds have a higher volatile oil content compared to other parts of the plant, making them ideal for creating tinctures that target nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and stems in the morning.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka.
Transfer the mixture to a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and label it.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for nausea are concentrated liquid extracts derived from plants, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. These natural remedies work by soothing the stomach, calming digestive issues, and promoting overall well-being. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with nausea include Ginger, Peppermint, and Lemon Balm. When taken as directed, these tinctures can provide rapid relief from morning sickness, motion sickness, and chemotherapy-induced nausea, allowing individuals to regain control over their daily lives and enjoy a better quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as diarrhea, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal remedies like peppermint, ginger, and dandelion root have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms. Additionally, tinctures made from herbs like chamomile, lemon balm, and passionflower can also alleviate anxiety and stress-related discomforts, which often manifest as nausea-like symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nausea with herbal tinctures is to combine a few specific remedies. Peppermint oil and ginger tincture are natural anti-emetics that help alleviate nausea symptoms. Take 10-20 drops of each tincture in water or tea, three times a day, as needed. Additionally, licorice root tincture helps soothe the digestive tract and reduces inflammation. Mix it with peppermint oil and ginger for a triple-therapy approach to effectively treat nausea.
